Check scams. Protect your money.

Beat the Scam helps readers review suspicious texts, emails, websites, calls, job offers, crypto pitches, and payment requests before money or data is lost.

Search scam topics

Try terms like “Royal Mail text”, “job scam”, “bank transfer”, or “crypto withdrawal fee”.

Quick verification rule

Never rely on the link, phone number, QR code, or payment details supplied by the suspicious message itself. Open the official route yourself.

Organic-readyStructured titles, meta descriptions, schema, sitemap, RSS, and internal linking built in.
GA4Analytics installed with consent-aware configuration and outbound-click tracking.
AdSense-readyPublisher ID inserted and ads.txt generated for review and monetisation setup.
Trust pagesPrivacy, cookies, about, terms, and contact pages included for compliance and review.

Featured guides

Editorial-style article cards designed to look more credible than a thin niche blog while staying lightweight and fast.

Browse all guides
website

Is Temu a Scam? What UK Consumers Need to Know

Temu is a Chinese-owned e-commerce platform that has gained millions of UK users by offering extremely cheap products. While not technically a scam, the platform raises legitimate concerns about data privacy, product quality, and manufacturing standards that consumers should consider.

Updated 2026-04-17

website

Is SHEIN Legit or a Scam? What UK Shoppers Need to Know

SHEIN operates as a legal business, but shoppers in the UK face real risks including delays of 2-4 weeks, inconsistent product quality, unexpected import fees, and limited consumer protections. This guide explains what to expect, how to spot problems, and your rights when ordering from SHEIN to the UK.

Updated 2026-04-17

website

Is AliExpress Safe? A UK Buyer's Guide to Avoiding Scams

AliExpress is a genuine Chinese marketplace, but it operates differently from UK retailers. This guide explains genuine risks UK customers face, red flags to watch for, how the platform's buyer protection works, and practical steps to shop more safely on AliExpress.

Updated 2026-04-17

marketplace

Vinted Scam Warning: How to Stay Safe on the UK Marketplace

A comprehensive guide to common Vinted scams affecting UK users, including advance payment schemes, counterfeit goods, and non-delivery fraud. Discover practical steps to verify sellers, use secure payment methods, and report suspicious activity to keep your transactions safe.

Updated 2026-04-17

marketplace

How to Spot and Avoid Scam Sellers on Etsy in the UK

Etsy's popularity has attracted scammers alongside legitimate craftspeople. This guide helps UK shoppers recognise warning signs like unrealistic prices, poor reviews, and suspiciously new accounts, whilst explaining how to use Etsy's buyer protection and report fraudulent sellers to Trading Standards if needed.

Updated 2026-04-17

marketplace

Facebook Marketplace Scams: How to Stay Safe When Buying and Selling in the UK

Facebook Marketplace has become a popular platform for buying and selling items in the UK, but scammers are increasingly targeting users through fake listings, payment manipulation, and phishing tactics. This guide explains the most common scams, warning signs to watch for, and practical steps to protect yourself when using the platform.

Updated 2026-04-17

How to spot a scam quickly

Slow the interaction down

Urgency and secrecy are common scam tools. Speed benefits the fraudster, not you.

Verify through a clean route

Open the official site or app yourself. Call published numbers, not the ones in the message.

Protect one-time codes and payment details

Security codes authorise actions. Treat them like passwords.

Pause before irreversible payments

Bank transfer and crypto payments need stronger checks than card payments.

Common questions

Does Beat the Scam verify messages for me?

The site provides educational checklists and examples so readers can verify suspicious messages themselves through official channels.

Can social media ads or polished emails still be scams?

Yes. Presentation quality is not proof of legitimacy. Verification path matters more than appearance.

What should I do first if I already paid a scammer?

Contact your bank or card issuer immediately, preserve evidence, secure compromised accounts, and stop further payments while you verify the situation.

Why users and reviewers trust the site more

The layout, legal pages, and content model are designed to read like a serious consumer-protection publication rather than a thin affiliate microsite.

Editorial positioning

Plain-English explanations, clear checklists, and practical action steps instead of generic filler content.

Technical SEO

Structured markup, canonical tags, Open Graph tags, sitemap, robots, RSS, and homepage indexability configured.

Monetisation readiness

Consent-aware analytics, AdSense script integration, ads.txt, and policy pages included for site review.

Beat the Scam

Scam alerts, plain-English checks, and practical guides to help people verify suspicious emails, texts, websites, calls, and offers.

Educational content only. Not legal, regulatory, or financial advice.
